reasoning: Peer sheet is classic Denzi dungeon tileset: cool grey/teal/dark palette, heavy black pixel outlines on every element, high detail density with dithered stone textures, directional shadows, muted color temperature throughout. Candidate panel_dialog_v01 fails on multiple style axes independent of item-type: (1) Color temperature — candidate is warm gold/cream/amber while entire peer set is cool-grey/dark; this is a palette identity failure, not an item-type allowance. (2) Palette overlap — near zero; peers use charcoal, slate, teal, dark crimson; candidate uses gold (#C8A840 range), ivory, warm brown — no shared hues. (3) Outline weight/color — peers uniformly use bold black pixel outlines; candidate substitutes a decorative golden border frame with no black silhouette outline, creating a fundamentally different visual grammar. (4) Detail density — peers are texture-dense with micro-pixel work filling every cell; candidate panels are mostly flat fill with sparse ornament. (5) Shadow — peers show consistent drop/inner shadows; candidate shows minimal to no shadowing. The golden urn ornaments and corner rosettes have competent pixel craft but still in a warm heraldic register alien to the Denzi dungeon palette. Charm dimension: peers are utilitarian-grim, not cute-Echo register; candidate is decorative-elegant but still not Echo-cute — neutral/no penalty here. Overall: too many style-axis failures across color, outline, density, and shadow to pass even allowing for UI-panel item-type latitude.
